Vaccine Providers - Resources & Training
Vaccine Storage & Handling
It’s very important to store and handle vaccines the right way. Vaccines can lose their power if they get too hot or too cold. This can make them not work well when people need them most. That’s why we have created a list of helpful resources and training to make sure everyone knows how to keep vaccines safe.
EZIZ.org
- California’s official resource hub for vaccine storage, handling, training, and immunization guidance—operated by the California Department of Public Health’s Immunization Branch. See resources and training from EZIZ below:
CA VFC Program's POM - IMM-1248.pdf
| Chapter 3 – Vaccine Management

CDPH Vaccine Requirements and Recommendations for Schools
In California, children need certain vaccines to enter school or childcare. As a vaccine provider, you help make sure children get the vaccines they need to stay healthy and meet state requirements. We’ve put together resources and training to help you understand California’s school and childcare vaccine rules.

School Immunization Law
We’re Here to Help
Making sure children are fully vaccinated and that vaccines are stored and handled correctly is an important job — and we’re here to support you every step of the way. Whether you have questions about vaccine requirements for school and childcare, or need help with proper vaccine storage and handling, our team is ready to assist. Please email us anytime if you need support or guidance –
vaccinehelp@edcgov.us
.
